Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-11 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Long-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns from 1 month to 10 years, with relative performance vs SPY over 1y/5y/10y. Same scoring engine as the short-term ranker but optimized for longer investment horizons and secular trends.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker-long Today's data shows CLMT ranked #1 with an exceptional 91.5 score driven by extraordinary short-to-medium term returns (143% in 1 year, 596% in 5 years), though this comes with a significant caveat: the stock has underperformed SPY by 34% over the past decade and dramatically underperformed QQQ by 273% over the same period, suggesting this is a recent momentum spike rather than sustained outperformance. IAU (gold) appears as the second candidate with more balanced long-term credibility—it shows consistent momentum ratios across all timeframes (all ratio_passes=5) and actually outperformed SPY and QQQ over 10 years (+54% vs SPY), making it a more stable long-horizon choice despite lower absolute returns. CLMT's "strong_buy" flag and perfect SMA alignment (price above all moving averages) indicate technical strength, but the 0.77 bad_spy_score and negative 10-year relative performance suggest this momentum may be cyclical or sector-specific (its positive correlations with energy/commodities via XLE/DBA support this), whereas IAU's diversified positive returns across decades suggest more fundamental appeal. The contrast between these two top-ranked tickers illustrates the dashboard's dual nature:

Click column headers to sort  |  Score = percentile composite: returns + ratio passes + bad-SPY resilience  |  SMA ✓ = price above all SMAs (30/50/100/200)  |  Ratio = short-term momentum consistency (out of 5 tests)  |  M = momentum signal (sortable) —  ● clean  ● stalling  ● overextended  |  V = volume confirmation (sortable) —  ● confirmed  ● normal  ○ thin  |  PB = entry timing: down day on strong stock (sortable by 1d return) —  ● buy today  ○ none  |  OL = theme overlap vs owned tickers —  ● overlap  ○ clear
